methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 24 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
BlackSea Technologies is a platform-stage defense and security company incubated through Razor's Edge Ventures. The valuation landscape includes a universe of 24 same-sector peers, comprising 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers including Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, Shield AI, and Chaos Industries.

A peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient narrow-stage comparables. Despite the 24-company sector universe, no same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier peers met the minimum threshold of three datapoints required for reliable median pricing. This absence reflects the heterogeneity within defense and security investing, where company trajectories, technology maturity, and customer acquisition patterns diverge significantly even within the same sector.

Without a triangulation result, a defensible valuation range cannot be established from the provided data. The public comp tickers and named private peers (Anduril, Shield AI, et al.) represent the available reference set, but without stage-matched multiples, revenue-based metrics, or explicit growth comparables, quantitative synthesis is not supported. Key uncertainties include BlackSea's revenue scale, capital efficiency relative to the peer set, and whether it should anchor to defense-focused peers or broader security technology valuations. Additional financial metrics—ARR, growth rates, or customer concentration—would be required to triangulate defensible valuation bounds.
